title: HTTP Methods: Detailed look at POST & PUT and where to use them

content.problemStatement:
In this lesson, we'll explore two important HTTP methods: POST and PUT. These methods are crucial for sending data to the server and creating or updating resources.

POST Method:
The POST method is used to submit data to be processed by the server. It's commonly used for:
1. Submitting form data
2. Creating new resources on the server
3. Sending data that doesn't fit well in the URL

In Flask, you can handle POST requests using the @app.route decorator with the methods parameter:

```python
@app.route('/submit', methods=['POST'])
def submit_data():
    # Handle the POST request here
    return "Data received"
```

PUT Method:
The PUT method is used to update existing resources on the server. It's typically used when:
1. Updating an entire resource
2. Replacing existing data with new data

Here's how you can handle PUT requests in Flask:

```python
@app.route('/update/', methods=['PUT'])
def update_data(id):
    # Handle the PUT request here
    return f"Data updated for ID: {id}"
```

When to use POST vs PUT:
- Use POST when creating new resources or submitting form data
- Use PUT when updating existing resources or replacing data

Remember, in your Flask routes, you can specify multiple HTTP methods for a single route:

```python
@app.route('/resource', methods=['GET', 'POST', 'PUT'])
def handle_resource():
    if request.method == 'GET':
        # Handle GET request
    elif request.method == 'POST':
        # Handle POST request
    elif request.method == 'PUT':
        # Handle PUT request
```

This approach allows you to handle different HTTP methods for the same URL, making your API more RESTful and following best practices for web development.

### Note
HTML forms only support `GET` and `POST` methods natively. \
To learn more about `PUT` - we will need working knowledge of JavaScript - this will be covered later.
